p.strong: Pointer at offset 3 → Read from aligned base → Mask bytes 0-2

div.p: Key Safety Property: The aligned address (addr & ~15) is always in the same page as the original pointer. Proof: Alignment adjustment is at most 15 bytes, which is less than page size (4096). Therefore: The 16-byte aligned load cannot cross a page boundary.
offset = addr & 15 = 3 ignore_mask = (1 << offset) - 1 = (1 << 3) - 1 = 0x07 = bits 0,1,2 set (IGNORE)
Result: Valid bytes start at bit 0 Position of first valid byte = __builtin_ctz(mask) = 0
